quality_summary.notes: Lecture deck imported cleanly in fixture mode

slides.title: Backpropagation Essentials
slides.snippet_text: Learning objectives: explain the chain rule, compute a backward pass, and apply gradient descent updates.
bullets: Explain why gradients are propagated from the loss backward
Connect local derivatives to global updates
Identify the role of the learning rate

slides.title: Chain Rule Intuition
slides.snippet_text: The chain rule breaks a complicated derivative into local derivatives that can be multiplied along the computation path.
bullets: Write the output as a composition of intermediate functions
Differentiate one edge at a time
Multiply local gradients to get the total derivative

slides.title: Forward Pass and Backward Pass
slides.snippet_text: Store intermediate activations during the forward pass so the backward pass can reuse them when computing gradients.
bullets: Forward pass computes activations and loss
Backward pass reuses cached activations
Each layer passes gradient information to the previous layer

slides.title: Gradient Descent Update
slides.snippet_text: Parameter update: w <- w - eta * dL/dw. The minus sign matters because we are descending the loss surface.
bullets: Use the learning rate eta to control step size
Subtract the gradient to reduce the loss
Check tensor shapes before applying the update

slides.title: Common Mistakes
slides.snippet_text: Common errors include dropping an intermediate derivative, reversing the sign of the update, and ignoring dimension checks.
bullets: Do not skip intermediate terms in the chain rule
Do not add the gradient when the slide uses subtraction
Verify activation and weight matrix dimensions

quality_summary.notes: Short student notes with a few paraphrases

slides.title: Student recap
slides.snippet_text: Remember to save intermediate activations, keep the minus sign in the update, and check each derivative term.
bullets: Cache activations in the forward pass
Track the sign on the learning update
Compare each step with the slide deck

quality_summary.notes: Template favors short prompts followed by concise rubric bullets

slides.title: Template structure
slides.snippet_text: Each question begins with an imperative verb and expects two to three compact supporting points.
bullets: Explain / Compare / Derive prompt style
Compact answer format
Rubric awards points for named steps

messages.text: Why does the update use subtraction instead of addition?

reply_sections.heading: Lecture-grounded explanation
reply_sections.text: The lecture slide writes the update as w <- w - eta * dL/dw, which means the parameter moves opposite the gradient so the loss decreases rather than increases.

citations.snippet_text: Subtract the gradient to reduce the loss.

questions.stem: Explain how the chain rule supports backpropagation through a multi-layer network.
questions.expected_answer: A strong answer states that the total derivative is built by multiplying local derivatives along the computation path, usually mentioning intermediate activations or nodes.

rubric.description: Names local derivatives and explains that they are multiplied
Links the chain rule directly to gradient flow through layers

questions.scoring_guide_text: Full credit requires both the mathematical idea and the network-specific interpretation.

citations.snippet_text: Multiply local gradients to get the total derivative.

questions.stem: Walk through a forward pass and backward pass, explaining why cached activations are needed in the backward computation.
questions.expected_answer: A strong answer describes the forward pass computing activations and the loss, then explains that the backward pass uses those stored activations to compute gradients layer by layer.

rubric.description: Explains what is computed and saved
Explains how saved activations support gradient computation

questions.scoring_guide_text: Look for explicit mention of reusing forward-pass information during the backward pass.

citations.snippet_text: Store intermediate activations during the forward pass so the backward pass can reuse them.

questions.stem: State the gradient descent update rule from the lecture and explain the role of the minus sign.
questions.expected_answer: The answer should give w <- w - eta * dL/dw and say the minus sign moves parameters opposite the gradient so the loss decreases.

rubric.description: Explains that subtraction corresponds to descending the loss

questions.scoring_guide_text: Students should connect the sign to optimization, not only memorize the formula.

citations.snippet_text: Parameter update: w <- w - eta * dL/dw.

coverage_report.notes: The fixture practice set concentrates on the core worked-example slides rather than the title slide.
